# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Pod specification for our container that needs to interact with the Docker socket for CI/CD operations. The pod needs elevated permissions to build and push Docker images. This runs in our build cluster for automated deployments.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v1   # API version of your Pod specification (v1) 
kind: Pod     # Type is defined as a 'Pod' here   
metadata:       # Metadata goes inside this section if any specific name needs to be given for the pod, otherwise it will have auto-generated one. If you want unique names then use `name` field below  
  labels:       
      purpose: ci/cd         # This would define a 'label' which can later reference when creating services or other resources from this Pod specification (e.g., by selector)    
manifests:       # Container and image details, these go inside here   
- name: myapp           # Name of the Docker container in your pod 
   imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent       
   image: <DOCKER_IMAGE>  # Specify docker Image where you want to run on kubernetes cluster (<YOUR DOCKER IMAGES NAME>)   
   command:                   # This is the entry point for your application, in our case it will be 'sh' which runs shell script. If needed more details then use `args` field below    
      - '/bin/bash'             
      - '-c'                   
      - './script_to_build_and_push_image.sh'"       # It should contain your build and push image commands (<BUILD AND PUSH IMAGE SCRIPT>)   
   securityContext:          # Security related properties can be added here, like 'capabilities', etc    
      runAsUser: 1000           
volumesMounts:           # This is where volume information for the Pod goes. volumes and mountpoints are used to provide data in or out of a container  
- name: sharedVolume         # Name that will be given to this Volume (shared with other pod, e.g., nginx)    
  mountPath: /mnt/data       # Path where volume can exist within the Pod   
 - name: configMapData        # Another way of referring by its label  
      ConfigMapKeyRef:        
          name: my-configmap           # Name that maps to a key in your configuration map (my-configmaps)    
            key: settings.txt 
envVars:                  # These are environment variables for the application, if needed then they will be added here   
 - name: MY_VAR                # Names of this variable  
       valueFrom:              # Source from which to take an input (defaults To Secret or ConfigMap)    
          secretKeyRef:         # Name that maps a key in your secrets and configmaps if needed then provide it below.  It will look for 'MY_VAR' inside the my-secrets/mydata   
              name: MY_SECRETS  
                key: var1
